title: ESM Packaging, Exports, Executable Entry, Artifact Inspection, and Platform Support · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for esm packaging, exports, executable entry, artifact inspection, and platform support and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, compile diagnostics, explicit non-claims, and named browser, TypeScript, Node, network, filesystem, terminal, load, accessibility, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for installable inspected Node Pokedex package. Learners progress through pre…
objectives: Define package type, source and output roots, explicit extensions, imports, exports, internal boundaries, and supported Node resolution.
Publish an executable JavaScript entry with portable launcher behavior, thin process wiring, correct permissions, and no source-tree assumptions.
Use package files or ignore rules, inspect npm pack output, include license and docs, exclude secrets and residue, and verify dependency provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Pokedex Ts Esm Boundaries Pokedex product engineering and TypeScript HTTP evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Pokedex Ts Esm Boundaries assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A quiz TypeScript team handles case 7440 during an out-of-order Promise fault rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bind ESM source and emitted JavaScript to inspected packages, executable smoke, and platform behavior; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, and packed artifact; then change one compiler, Node runtime, emitted artifact, package, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define package type, source and output roots, explicit extensions, imports, exports, internal boundaries, and supported Node resolution.
